message:
feat(messaging): full FABRIC IR compilation with artifact registry and service/morphism/protocol/consumer compilation

Expand the FabricCompiler from subject-only cost estimation to full
IR-wide compilation covering all declaration families.

New types (+~600 lines):
- CompiledArtifactKind: 10 artifact kinds (Subject, Morphism, Service,
  Protocol, Consumer, PrivacyPolicy, CutPolicy, BranchPolicy,
  ObligationContract, CapabilityToken)
- CompiledArtifact: kind + name + dependency keys + cost envelope
- CompilerWarning: non-fatal field-level findings for operators

FabricCompileReport expansion:
- artifacts: Vec<CompiledArtifact> for all compiled declarations
- warnings: Vec<CompilerWarning> for non-fatal diagnostics
- aggregate_cost now spans all artifact kinds, not just subjects

Compilation methods:
- compile_subjects(): subject family cost estimation (existing)
- compile_morphisms(): morphism transform + privacy + evidence validation
  with dependency tracking to source/target subjects
- compile_services(): service contract validation with capability
  cross-reference checking and timeout/TTL cost estimation
- compile_protocols(): session-typed protocol contract compilation
  with role count and step depth cost estimation
- compile_consumers(): consumer policy compilation with ack-policy
  and max-deliver cost adjustments
- compile_privacy_policies(): standalone privacy policy validation
- compile_cut_policies(): cut/checkpoint policy compilation
- compile_branch_policies(): counterfactual branch validation
- compile_obligation_contracts(): quantitative obligation compilation
- compile_capability_tokens(): capability permission cost estimation

Tests (+~550 lines):
- Full IR round-trip compilation with all declaration families
- Morphism dependency tracking and privacy/evidence warnings
- Service capability cross-reference validation
- Protocol role count and step depth cost estimation
- Consumer ack-policy cost adjustment verification
- Empty IR produces zero-cost baseline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
